parameters.name: DATA_WIDTH
parameters.description: AXI data width in bits; baseline PL330-like engineering subset uses 64-bit memory beats.

parameters.name: ADDR_WIDTH
parameters.description: AXI/APB-visible address width in bits for SAR/DAR/PC fields.

parameters.name: ID_WIDTH
parameters.description: AXI ID width for read/write master channels.

parameters.name: NUM_CHANNELS
parameters.description: Number of DMA channels implemented in this verification target.

parameters.name: MAX_BURST_LEN
parameters.description: Maximum AXI burst length in beats for the baseline contract.

parameters.name: CLOCK_FREQ_MHZ
parameters.description: Target clock frequency for timing and cycle-model assumptions.

parameters.name: RESET_POLARITY
parameters.description: Reset polarity fixed by dmacresetn naming and reset scheme.

parameters.name: SUPPORT_UNALIGNED
parameters.description: When zero, unaligned SAR/DAR on start raises ERR_UNALIGNED instead of issuing AXI traffic.

sub_modules.name: pl330realverify_regs
sub_modules.description: APB register decode, CSR storage, W1C interrupt/status behavior, and register-driven command pulses.

sub_modules.name: pl330realverify_channel_fsm
sub_modules.description: Per-channel control FSM for start, WFP, AXI read/write sequencing, completion, and fault transitions.

sub_modules.name: pl330realverify_axi_rd
sub_modules.description: AXI read address/data protocol adapter and read response classifier.

sub_modules.name: pl330realverify_axi_wr
sub_modules.description: AXI write address/data/response protocol adapter and write response classifier.

sub_modules.name: pl330realverify_datapath
sub_modules.description: Address counters, loop counters, read data buffer, write payload/strobe generation, and status updates.

sub_modules.name: pl330realverify_event_irq
sub_modules.description: Peripheral event selection, interrupt pending/enable combine, W1C clear, and dmac_irq generation.

fields.name: num_channels_minus1
fields.description: NUM_CHANNELS minus one for software discovery.

registers.name: DBGCMD
write_side_effects: A completing APB write with dbgcmd=0 emits one debug_execute pulse when the manager is not busy.

fields.name: dbgcmd
fields.write_effect: 0=execute debug command pulse; other values are ignored in this subset

fields.name: channel
fields.write_effect: Select target channel for debug execution when implemented

registers.name: INTEN
write_side_effects: Software writes update interrupt enable bits immediately; enabling an already-pending source may assert dmac_irq on the next cycle.

registers.name: INTSTATUS
registers.description: Raw interrupt status register with write-one-to-clear behavior.
write_side_effects: Writing one clears the corresponding pending interrupt bit; writing zero preserves the bit.

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: 0=no error, 1=debug reject, 2=unaligned, 3=AXI read error, 4=AXI write error, 5=event timeout.

fields.name: loop_remaining
fields.description: Remaining beats in the active transfer, saturated to eight status bits.

registers.name: SAR
write_side_effects: Software writes update the source address only while the channel is STOPPED or COMPLETED.

fields.name: src_addr
fields.write_effect: Update source byte address when channel is not EXECUTING or WAITING_FOR_PERIPHERAL

registers.name: DAR
write_side_effects: Software writes update the destination address only while the channel is STOPPED or COMPLETED.

fields.name: dst_addr
fields.write_effect: Update destination byte address when channel is not EXECUTING or WAITING_FOR_PERIPHERAL

registers.name: LOOP_CFG
registers.description: Per-channel transfer loop and burst configuration.
write_side_effects: Software writes update transfer count and burst shape while the channel is idle.

fields.name: loop_count
fields.write_effect: Encoded transfer count: zero means one beat, N means N+1 beats

fields.name: burst_len
fields.write_effect: Encoded AXI burst length: zero means one beat, N means N+1 beats

registers.name: CONTROL
write_side_effects: Writing start=1 while channel is STOPPED or COMPLETED accepts a transfer command and self-clears start.
Writing halt=1 while EXECUTING requests a graceful transition to STOPPED after the active beat.
fault_inject forces the declared fault path for verification.

fields.name: start
fields.write_effect: 1 launches a transfer command and the stored bit self-clears

fields.name: halt
fields.write_effect: 1 requests graceful halt and the stored bit self-clears

fields.name: wfp_enable
fields.write_effect: Enable wait-for-peripheral gating before read issue

fields.name: wfp_event
fields.write_effect: Select peripheral_events index used by WFP

fields.name: fault_inject
fields.write_effect: Force channel fault on accepted start for verification

registers.name: PC
registers.description: Instruction/program counter placeholder for PL330-like program-flow observability.
write_side_effects: Software writes update PC while the channel is STOPPED; this engineering subset does not fetch external microcode.

fields.name: pc_addr
fields.write_effect: Update observable program counter when idle

sources.name: DBG_DONE
sources.description: Debug command done or rejected status.

interrupts.clear_policy: Write one to INTSTATUS clears the selected raw pending bit; write zero preserves it.
interrupts.completion_policy: Completion and fault are mutually exclusive for a channel terminal event; first fault suppresses completion interrupt for that transfer.

rtl_contract.sample_condition: dmacresetn == 1 and psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 1 and paddr == 276 and (pwdata & 1) != 0

state_updates.name: sar
state_updates.expr: sar + (DATA_WIDTH // 8) if (bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp == 0) else sar

state_updates.name: dar
state_updates.expr: dar + (DATA_WIDTH // 8) if (bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp == 0) else dar

state_updates.name: loop_remaining
state_updates.expr: (loop_remaining - 1) if (bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp == 0 and loop_remaining > 0) else loop_remaining

state_updates.name: status
state_updates.expr: 8 if ((rvalid == 1 and rready == 1 and rresp != 0) or (bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp != 0)) else (6 if (bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp == 0 and loop_remaining <= 1) else status)

state_updates.name: error_code
state_updates.expr: 3 if (rvalid == 1 and rready == 1 and rresp != 0) else (4 if (bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp != 0) else error_code)

state_updates.name: intstatus
state_updates.expr: (intstatus | (1 if (bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp == 0 and loop_remaining <= 1) else 0) | (256 if ((rvalid == 1 and rready == 1 and rresp != 0) or (bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp != 0)) else 0)) & (~(pwdata if (psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 1 and paddr == 36) else 0))

rtl_contract.reserved_behavior: Reserved register fields read zero, ignore writes, and are not implemented as storage.
rtl_contract.illegal_access_behavior: Illegal APB accesses complete with pready=1 and pslverr=1 without changing DMA architectural state.

function_model.purpose: Executable cycle-independent behavioral contract for PL330-like DMA transfer, wait-for-peripheral, debug command, APB register side effects, interrupt clear, and fault behavior.

state_variables.name: sar
state_variables.description: Current source byte address for the selected channel.

state_variables.name: dar
state_variables.description: Current destination byte address for the selected channel.

state_variables.name: loop_remaining
state_variables.description: Number of transfer beats remaining, where encoded loop_count N loads N+1 beats.

state_variables.name: rd_buf
state_variables.description: Last accepted AXI read data beat.

state_variables.name: pc
state_variables.description: Observable PL330-like program/debug address; no external microcode fetch in this subset.

transactions.name: reset_architecture
outputs: All externally visible output valids and interrupt are deasserted during reset.
side_effects: All architectural state returns to declared reset values.

transactions.name: apb_register_write
preconditions: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 1 and pready == 1
outputs: pready acknowledges the access; pslverr reports illegal address/access/strobe.

state_updates.name: inten
state_updates.expr: ((inten & (~write_mask_32)) | (pwdata & write_mask_32)) if (apb_addr_inten == 1 and illegal_apb_access == 0) else inten

state_updates.name: intstatus
state_updates.expr: (intstatus & (~pwdata)) if (apb_addr_intstatus == 1 and illegal_apb_access == 0) else intstatus

state_updates.name: sar
state_updates.expr: pwdata if (apb_addr_sar == 1 and channel_idle == 1 and illegal_apb_access == 0) else sar

state_updates.name: dar
state_updates.expr: pwdata if (apb_addr_dar == 1 and channel_idle == 1 and illegal_apb_access == 0) else dar

side_effects: Writable register fields update only through declared write_effect rules; reserved fields ignore writes.

error_cases.result: pslverr asserted for the completing access; architectural DMA transfer state is not modified.

transactions.name: apb_register_read
preconditions: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 0 and pready == 1
outputs: prdata returns the decoded register value with reserved bits forced to zero.

state_updates.name: status
state_updates.expr: status

side_effects: APB reads do not alter architectural state.

transactions.name: single_or_multi_beat_memory_copy
outputs: Each successful beat writes the captured read data to the destination address.
Final successful beat sets status COMPLETED and raises the channel-complete pending bit.

state_updates.name: rd_buf
state_updates.expr: rdata if (rvalid == 1 and rready == 1 and rresp == 0) else rd_buf

state_updates.name: sar
state_updates.expr: sar + (DATA_WIDTH // 8)

state_updates.name: dar
state_updates.expr: dar + (DATA_WIDTH // 8)

state_updates.name: loop_remaining
state_updates.expr: max(loop_remaining - 1, 0)

state_updates.name: status
state_updates.expr: 6 if loop_remaining <= 1 else 1

state_updates.name: intstatus
state_updates.expr: intstatus | complete_irq_mask if loop_remaining <= 1 else intstatus

side_effects: SAR and DAR increment by DATA_WIDTH/8 bytes after each successful write response.
loop_remaining decrements after each successful write response.

error_cases.result: status=FAULTED, error_code=ERR_AXI_RD, CH_FAULT pending set, no write for the failed beat.

transactions.name: wait_for_peripheral_event
outputs: Channel remains WAITING_FOR_PERIPHERAL until the selected event bit is sampled high.

state_updates.name: status
state_updates.expr: 1 if selected_event == 1 else 2

state_updates.name: loop_remaining
state_updates.expr: loop_remaining

side_effects: No AXI transaction is issued while selected_event is zero.

transactions.name: fault_completion
outputs: Fault status is latched and a channel-fault pending interrupt is set.

state_updates.name: error_code
state_updates.expr: fault_code

state_updates.name: intstatus
state_updates.expr: intstatus | fault_irq_mask

side_effects: First fault wins until software clears INTSTATUS and restarts the channel.

error_cases.result: debug command rejected without transfer side effects.

transactions.name: interrupt_write_one_to_clear
preconditions: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 1 and pready == 1
outputs: dmac_irq reflects the enabled pending status after the W1C clear.

state_updates.name: intstatus
state_updates.expr: intstatus & (~pwdata)

side_effects: Only bits written as one are cleared; bits written as zero retain their prior value.

function_model.reference_model_hint: tb-gen should build a Python scoreboard from transactions, output_rules, state_updates, and invariants; prose outputs are explanatory only.

cycle_model.purpose: Cycle/handshake contract defining when PL330-like architectural state, AXI/APB valid-ready signals, interrupt outputs, and error status may change.
cycle_model.backend_policy: Use PyMTL3 or direct Python cycle model as a timing oracle; FunctionalModel remains the behavioral oracle.

reset.assertion: dmacresetn low asynchronously clears architectural state and deasserts valid/ready output valids and dmac_irq.
reset.deassertion: State is usable on the first rising dmaclk edge after synchronized deassertion.

register_read.description: pready/prdata/pslverr complete in APB access phase.

register_write.description: Writable side effects occur on the completing APB access cycle.

event_release.description: Selected peripheral event sampled then WFP state can advance.

single_beat_transfer.description: Command accept, AR, R, AW/W, and B stages; max is unbounded under AXI backpressure.

interrupt_assertion.description: Pending enabled interrupt reflects terminal status or W1C clear within two cycles.

handshake_rules.rule: pready asserts only for psel==1 and penable==1 and completes exactly one APB transfer.
prdata is stable for the completing read access and reserved bits read zero.
handshake_rules.sample_condition: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 0 and pready == 1

handshake_rules.rule: Hold arvalid and AR payload stable until arvalid and arready are sampled high.
Assert rready only when rd_buf can accept rdata; capture exactly one beat on rvalid and rready.
Hold awvalid and AW payload stable until awvalid and awready are sampled high.
Hold wvalid, wdata, wstrb, and wlast stable until wvalid and wready are sampled high.
Assert bready while waiting for write response; consume bresp on bvalid and bready.
dmac_irq equals the OR-reduction of intstatus & inten after reset, terminal updates, and W1C clears settle.

pipeline.action: Decode APB access, update configuration or emit start/halt/debug pulses.
Latch channel command, SAR, DAR, count, WFP configuration, and error precheck results.
If WFP is enabled, hold state until selected peripheral event samples high.
Drive AXI AR payload and hold until AR handshake.
Accept AXI R beat, classify rresp, and capture rdata into rd_buf.
Drive AXI AW and W payloads; each channel may complete independently but both must handshake before B wait.
Consume AXI B response, update address/count/status/interrupts.
Post COMPLETED or FAULTED status and level interrupt state.

ordering: A write data beat must not be issued before the corresponding read data beat has been accepted into rd_buf.
A channel-complete interrupt pending bit is set only after the final successful B response.
A channel-fault interrupt pending bit is set on the first detected fault before any later completion status.
APB W1C clear does not clear configuration registers or address counters.
For each channel, at most one read burst and one write burst are outstanding in this engineering subset.
backpressure: If arready is zero, AR payload remains stable and no read transaction is counted.
If rvalid is zero, rd_buf and downstream write stage remain stable.
If awready or wready is zero, write payload/address/control remain stable until handshakes occur.
If bvalid is zero, architectural completion/fault for that write is delayed.
APB accesses are not backpressured in the baseline beyond the single access phase.

throughput.condition: No APB command gap, WFP disabled or already satisfied, no AXI backpressure, no AXI errors, aligned addresses.

outstanding.description: Single outstanding read/write in the engineering subset.

depth.description: Visible cycle-model pipeline and buffering depth.

observability: Every function_model transaction maps to one or more cycle_model stages and at least one coverage bin.
Waveform debug must show state, start_cmd, selected_event, AXI handshakes, rd_buf, status, error_code, intstatus, inten, and dmac_irq.

transitions.description: Wait for selected peripheral event before issuing memory traffic.

transitions.condition: bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp == 0 and loop_remaining == 1
bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp == 0 and loop_remaining > 1

transitions.description: Return to idle-visible stopped state after software observes completion or starts a new command.

command_path.source: APB writes to CONTROL.start or DBGCMD
command_path.sequence: APB_ACCESS -> start/debug pulse -> channel_fsm command accept -> command snapshot

read_path.burst: INCR burst, arlen from LOOP_CFG.burst_len capped by MAX_BURST_LEN, arsize=clog2(DATA_WIDTH/8)
read_path.sequence: SAR -> AR payload -> R handshake -> rd_buf
read_path.error_exit: Nonzero rresp raises ERR_AXI_RD and blocks the corresponding write.

write_path.burst: INCR burst with AW/W payload generated after read data capture; wstrb all ones for DATA_WIDTH bytes.
write_path.sequence: rd_buf -> AW/W payload -> B response -> SAR/DAR/loop update
write_path.error_exit: Nonzero bresp raises ERR_AXI_WR and suppresses completion interrupt.

loop_control.terminal: Completion is posted when the successful write response consumes the final beat.
loop_control.auto_increment: SAR += DATA_WIDTH/8 and DAR += DATA_WIDTH/8 per successful beat.

event_path.sequence: sample selected event -> release WAITING_FOR_PERIPHERAL -> issue read
event_path.integration_requirement: External event sources must be synchronous to dmaclk or held for one dmaclk sample.

interrupt_path.source: terminal channel events, fault classifier, debug done, APB W1C clear
interrupt_path.sequence: event -> INTSTATUS bit set/clear -> AND with INTEN -> OR-reduce to dmac_irq

debug_path.sequence: APB write DBGCMD -> debug_execute pulse if manager idle -> parameterized status/IRQ update through INTEN.dbg_done_en
debug_path.scope: Instruction RAM/fetch/decode is not modeled in this engineering subset; PC is observable configuration state.

error_sources.architectural_effect: error_code=1, debug command rejected, DBG_DONE pending set when INTEN.dbg_done_en is enabled
status=FAULTED, error_code=2, CH_FAULT pending set, no AXI issue
status=FAULTED, error_code=3, CH_FAULT pending set, no destination write for failed beat
status=FAULTED, error_code=4, CH_FAULT pending set, completion suppressed
pslverr=1 for that access; DMA channel architectural state unchanged

propagation: First channel fault wins until software clears INTSTATUS and restarts/reinitializes the channel.
A fault terminal event sets CH_FAULT and suppresses CH_COMPLETE for the same transfer.
AXI read error prevents the corresponding write beat from being issued.
APB illegal access error is reported on pslverr but does not mutate transfer state.

clears: dmac_irq when no enabled pending bits remain
CSR.error_code on accepted new legal start
preserves: global interrupt enables unless explicitly written
